descriptions: to make something very unpleasant happen to someone
examples: Violence was relentlessly visited upon innocent victims.
highlighted_examples: Violence was relentlessly [visited] upon innocent victims.

descriptions: to give a person or political party a position of power by voting
examples: 'Would you consider changing your vote in order to... simplify... to simplify... Dr Moon!
We’re the ones who voted him into office.
Would people be willing to vote the Tories in again?
highlighted_examples: 'Would you consider changing your [vote] [in] order to... simplify... to simplify... Dr Moon!
We’re the ones who [voted] him into office.
Would people be willing to [vote] the Tories [in] again?

descriptions: to remove someone from a position on something such as a committee by voting
examples: They’ve voted him off the board.

descriptions: to make something accepted officially by voting
examples: We need to get the budget voted through.
highlighted_examples: We need to get the budget [voted] [through].
